testing_in_progress.focus: Mid-range hotspot weight reduction for contested junction flexibility
testing_in_progress.description: Reduced mid-range hotspot_weight from 5.7 to 5.6 (-1.8%) in scoring.py
testing_in_progress.rationale: Builds on attempts 063 (5.8→5.7, -1.7%) and 046 (6.0→5.8, -3.3%) for continued mid-range contested junction optimization. Synergizes with near-hub hotspot reduction (045, 064) and far-range hotspot reductions (051, 062, 079) for comprehensive hotspot weight optimization across all hub distance ranges. Attempt 021 showed reducing base from 12.0→11.0 failed (-9.8%), but conservative incremental reductions targeting specific distance ranges have been successful. Progressive mid-range hotspot weight reductions from 6.0 to 5.6 enable increasingly flexible mid-range contested junction pursuit.
testing_in_progress.note: Forty-second scissors improvement. Continues mid-range hotspot optimization (046, 063, 080).

last_improvement.focus: Network bonus increase for chain-building consolidation
last_improvement.description: Increased network_bonus from 0.5 to 0.75 (+50%) on top of 014+015+016 stack
last_improvement.result: IMPROVEMENT: gamma_v6:v1 avg 15.84 vs gamma_v5:v1 baseline 15.25 (+3.9%)
last_improvement.notes: CORRECTED ASSESSMENT: Early 12-match sample showed -20% regression (13.48 avg), but with 27 matches shows +3.9% improvement. Small tournament samples misleading. Network bonus helps chain-building without over-clustering. Stack now: 014+015+016+018. Rank #9, best performing configuration.

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM prompt with prescriptive anti-stagnation role-change rules
failed_attempts.result: REGRESSION: 3.52 vs baseline 6.03 (-41.6%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Too aggressive role switching disrupted team stability. Prescriptive rules (stalled miner→aligner, stalled aligner→miner) caused excessive role churn. May need softer approach or better stall detection threshold.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced early phase from 30 steps to 15 steps to get 5 aligners faster
failed_attempts.result: REGRESSION: 5.67 vs baseline 6.03 (-5.97%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Ramping too early before economy stabilizes. Agents likely ran out of hearts/resources before establishing mining base. 30-step buffer seems necessary for resource bootstrapping.

failed_attempts.description: Removed corner_pressure bonus from scramble targeting to make scramblers more defensive
failed_attempts.result: SLIGHT REGRESSION: 8.96 vs baseline 9.03 (-0.77%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Minimal impact. Corner pressure bonus (~10 points max) may actually help in 4-team by encouraging scramblers to disrupt distant enemies. Defensive scrambling doesn't improve over existing threat_bonus prioritization.

failed_attempts.description: Increased network bonus weight from 0.5 to 1.5 (3×) to prioritize chain-building near existing friendly junctions
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: 3.23 vs baseline 9.03 (-64.2%)
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO AGGRESSIVE. Tripling network bonus caused agents to cluster around existing junctions instead of expanding. Leads to stagnant territory and missed opportunities. Expansion bonus (max 30) balanced against network bonus (max 2) for a reason - need both expansion and consolidation.

failed_attempts.description: Increased scrambler threat_bonus weight from 10.0 to 15.0 to better defend friendly junctions in 4-team format
failed_attempts.result: REGRESSION: 8.08 vs baseline 9.74 (-17.04%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Increasing defensive priority hurt performance. Scramblers became too focused on protecting existing junctions rather than disrupting enemy expansion. Junction collapse pattern not addressed by over-defending. Current 10.0 weight appears optimal.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced _TARGET_CLAIM_STEPS from 30 to 20 for faster claim turnover in 4-team high-churn environment
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: 4.58 vs baseline 9.74 (-53.0%)
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO SHORT. Reducing claim duration from 30 to 20 steps caused massive duplication. Agents re-claim same junctions multiple times before teammate arrives, wasting coordination. 30-step window (6 sec) is necessary for agents to actually reach claimed targets. Shorter claims = more churn, not better efficiency.

failed_attempts.description: Changed pressure_budget=6 threshold from step 3000 to step 2000 to get 5 aligners + 1 scrambler earlier during critical window
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: 5.15 vs baseline 9.74 (-47.13%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Ramping to 6 pressure budget 1000 steps earlier caused significant performance drop. Agents likely exhausted hearts/resources before establishing sustainable economy. Similar to failed attempt 003 (30→15 steps, -5.97%). Pattern confirms: premature pressure ramps hurt economy. Critical window 500-2000 refers to when to capitalize on existing resources, not when to ramp resource burn.

failed_attempts.description: Increased RETREAT_MARGIN from 15 to 20 to match alpha.0's more conservative retreat threshold for better agent survival
failed_attempts.result: SLIGHT REGRESSION: 9.34 vs baseline 9.74 (-4.0%), but EXTREME INSTABILITY with variance 6.63
failed_attempts.analysis: UNACCEPTABLE VARIANCE despite small regression. Individual seeds ranged from -79% to +125% (42: +107%, 43: -79%, 44: -38%, 45: +125%, 46: +24%). More conservative retreat creates unpredictable behavior: some scenarios agents survive longer for massive gains, other scenarios agents retreat too early for major losses. Similar to attempt 012 (+3.8% avg but 40% catastrophic failure rate). Standard deviation 6.63 (similar to baseline 6.61) but extreme individual swings make this unsuitable for production. RETREAT_MARGIN=15 appears better calibrated for four_score dynamics than alpha.0's machina_1-tuned value.

failed_attempts.focus: Nearby teammate role awareness for LLM
failed_attempts.description: Added nearby teammate role counts (≤20 tiles) to LLM analysis prompt to detect clustering and suggest role switches
failed_attempts.result: UNSTABLE: avg 10.11 vs baseline 9.74 (+3.8%), but variance 22.14 with 40% catastrophic failure rate
failed_attempts.analysis: EXTREME INSTABILITY. Despite slight average improvement, 2 of 5 seeds had catastrophic failures (1.57, 2.43). LLM role suggestions based on nearby teammates appear to trigger pathological behavior in some conditions, possibly excessive role switching or deadlock. 40% failure rate unacceptable for production. Nearby coordination awareness needs different approach - perhaps non-LLM heuristic or less aggressive prompting.

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM analysis prompt with explicit Stalled/Oscillating definitions, suggestive examples, and 'STRONGLY PREFER null' emphasis
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: 5.91 vs baseline 9.74 (-39.4%)
failed_attempts.analysis: WORSE THAN PRESCRIPTIVE APPROACH. Attempt to soften failed attempt 002's prescriptive rules (-41.6%) resulted in similar magnitude regression (-39.4%). Adding detailed explanations, examples, and strong 'prefer null' bias actually degraded performance vs simpler prompt. LLM may be confused by verbose guidance or 'STRONGLY PREFER null' is too restrictive. Pattern suggests: LLM role guidance itself may be problematic regardless of phrasing - both prescriptive (002: -41.6%) and suggestive (this: -39.4%) approaches fail. Consider abandoning LLM-driven role changes entirely or use only for extreme dysfunction detection with very high threshold.

failed_attempts.description: Fixed out-of-bounds exploration: reduced offset magnitudes (22→15), added dynamic flipping for corner detection. All roles now 100% valid exploration (was 63% OOB for aligners, 75% for miners/scramblers).
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: gamma_scissors:v1 avg 6.26 vs baseline 16.84 (-62.8%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Despite fixing OOB pathfinding issue, performance collapsed. Agents may have been over-exploring instead of capturing junctions, or reduced exploration range (22→15) limited territory discovery. Original exploration offsets (even with OOB failures) apparently worked better due to sticky targets keeping agents focused on valid junctions most of the time.

failed_attempts.focus: Hub penalty reduction for center control
failed_attempts.description: Reduced far-range hub_penalty multiplier from 8.0 to 6.0 (-25%) for junctions >25 distance to encourage center-map territory
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v2 avg 7.83 vs baseline 15.22 (-48.6%)
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO AGGRESSIVE. Reducing hub penalty caused agents to overextend to distant junctions, likely dying more often or wasting time on low-value targets. Hub-proximal strategy is core to success in four_score corner spawns. 8.0 multiplier is correctly calibrated.

failed_attempts.description: Increased teammate_penalty from 9.0 to 10.0 (+11%) to further reduce multi-agent duplication
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v3 avg 8.71 vs baseline 15.22 (-42.8%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Increasing teammate penalty beyond 9.0 hurt coordination. Agents may have avoided too many junctions when teammates were nearby, missing opportunities or creating gaps in coverage. 9.0 appears to be the optimal balance found in attempt 011.

failed_attempts.focus: Hotspot weight reduction with improved enemy avoidance
failed_attempts.description: Reduced base hotspot_weight from 12.0 to 11.0 (-8%) for far junctions
failed_attempts.result: REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v4 avg 13.73 vs baseline 15.22 (-9.8%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Early 4-match sample showed false positive (+9.7%, 16.70 avg, rank #7) but regressed with more matches. Reducing hotspot penalty caused agents to pursue contested junctions too aggressively. 12.0 base weight is correctly calibrated for four_score multi-team dynamics. Pattern confirms: small-sample tournament results can be misleading.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced mid-range hotspot_weight from 6.0 to 5.5 (-8%) for junctions at hub distance 10-15
failed_attempts.result: CANCELED: Built on failed attempt 021
failed_attempts.analysis: Attempt 022 (scissors_v1:v5) uploaded but not validated. Canceled after attempt 021 failed, as both reduce hotspot penalties. No need to wait for tournament results.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced mid-range (15-25 distance) hub_penalty multiplier from 3.0 to 2.7 (-10%)
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v6 avg 10.83 vs baseline 15.25 (-29.0%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Worse than attempt 019 (-48.6%). Even conservative -10% reduction to mid-tier hub penalty hurt badly. Hub-proximal strategy is fundamental to four_score success. All three hub penalty tiers (0.3, 1.5, 3.0, 8.0) are correctly calibrated and should not be reduced.

failed_attempts.description: Increased expansion bonus cap from 36.0 to 42.0 (+17%) to better value exceptional high-expansion junctions (7 unreachables vs 6)
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v7 avg 12.56 vs baseline 15.84 (-20.7%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Significant regression. Increasing expansion cap encouraged agents to pursue exceptional expansion opportunities that may have been riskier or further from optimal paths. Cap of 36 (6 unreachables) appears to be the sweet spot - it rewards good expansion without over-prioritizing extreme cases. Pattern: parameter increases that 'amplify' existing behavior don't always improve - the current calibration is often optimal.

failed_attempts.description: Increased _JUNCTION_AOE_RANGE from 10 to 12 (+20%) to align closer to alpha.0's wider enemy detection
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v9 avg 9.66 vs baseline 15.84 (-39.0%)
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. Increasing AOE detection radius by 20% caused severe performance degradation. Agents likely became too cautious, avoiding too many contested areas or retreating too often. This affected both aligner targeting (enemy_aoe penalty) and scrambler targeting (blocked_neutrals bonus). Pattern: spatial detection parameters are extremely sensitive - even 20% changes can break behavior. Alpha.0's radius 20 may be compensated by other parameters we don't have.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced corner_pressure divisor from 8.0 to 7.0 (-12.5% to divisor, +14% to bonus) to encourage more aggressive distant scrambling
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v10 avg 8.82 vs baseline 15.84 (-44.3%)
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. Increasing corner pressure bonus caused scramblers to over-prioritize distant targets, likely dying too often or missing nearby high-value targets. The current divisor 8.0 is correctly calibrated - distant targets (70+) getting 8.75-10 bonus vs 7.0-10 is already aggressive enough. Pattern: scrambler targeting extremely sensitive to changes. Both increasing defensive priority (008: -17%) and increasing distant aggression (026: -44%) failed badly.

failed_attempts.focus: Target switch threshold reduction for better adaptability
failed_attempts.description: Reduced _TARGET_SWITCH_THRESHOLD from 3.0 to 2.5 (-17%) to make agents switch targets more readily
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v11 avg 10.55 vs baseline 15.82 (-33.3%)
failed_attempts.analysis: MAJOR FAILURE. Reducing switch threshold caused agents to switch targets too frequently, likely disrupting the stability that sticky targets provide. Agents probably oscillated between targets or abandoned good targets prematurely. The current threshold 3.0 is correctly calibrated - it prevents oscillation while still allowing adaptation. Pattern: behavioral parameters as sensitive as scoring weights. docs/strategy.md lists 'Sticky targets: threshold 3.0' as 'What Works' for good reason.

failed_attempts.focus: Hotspot cap increase for contested junction avoidance
failed_attempts.description: Increased hotspot penalty cap from 3 to 4 scrambles to more strongly avoid frequently contested junctions
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v12 avg 10.42 vs baseline 15.75 (-33.8%)
failed_attempts.analysis: MAJOR FAILURE. Increasing hotspot cap caused agents to over-avoid contested junctions. Max penalty increase (36→48 for far junctions) likely made agents too cautious, missing valuable contested junctions that are worth fighting for despite scrambles. Current cap of 3 scrambles is correctly calibrated. Pattern: 11TH CONSECUTIVE FAILURE (019-028). ALL incremental parameter tuning attempts have failed. Current gamma_v6:v1 parameters appear OPTIMAL for this architecture. Further improvements likely require architectural changes, not parameter adjustments.

baseline_four_score.notes: With hotspot penalty (004) + early scrambler (007). Validated across 5 seeds. Conservative defensive tuning with earlier disruption in 4-team format.
